arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ رَفَعَهُ: أَنَّهُ كَانَ يُمْسِكُ عَنِ التَّلْبِيَةِ فِي الْعُمْرَةِ إِذَا اسْتَلَمَ الْحَجَرَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Ibn Abī Laylā → ‘Aṭā’ → Ibn ‘Abbās
english.text: Ibn ‘Abbās reported (marfu‘ - attributed to the Prophet): “He used to stop Talbiyah in ‘Umrah when he touched the Stone.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ آدَمَ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَسَنٌ وَزُهَيْرٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ لَبَّى فِي الْعُمْرَةِ حَتَّى اسْتَلَمَ الْحَجَرَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Yaḥyā ibn Ādam → Ḥasan and Zuhayr → Ibn Abī Laylā → ‘Aṭā’ → Ibn ‘Abbās
english.text: Ibn ‘Abbās reported that the Prophet ﷺ recited Talbiyah in ‘Umrah until he touched the Stone.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ جَدِّهِ قَالَ: اعْتَمَرَ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ثَلَاثَ عُمَرَ، كُلُّ ذَلِكَ لَا يَقْطَعُ التَّلْبِيَةَ حَتَّى يَسْتَلِمَ الْحَجَرَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ḥafṣ → Ḥajjāj → ‘Amr ibn Shu‘ayb → His Father → His Grandfather
english.text: His grandfather ('Abdullah ibn 'Amr) said: “The Prophet ﷺ performed three ‘Umrahs. In all of them, he did not stop Talbiyah until he touched the Stone.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ بِشْرٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: الْمُعْتَمِرُ يُمْسِكُ عَنِ التَّلْبِيَةِ إِذَا اسْتَلَمَ الْحَجَرَ، وَالْحَاجُّ إِذَا رَمَى الْجَمْرَةَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Mughīrah → Bishr → Sa‘īd ibn Jubayr → Ibn ‘Abbās
english.text: Ibn ‘Abbās said: “The Mu'tamir (performer of ‘Umrah) stops Talbiyah when he touches the Stone, and the Haji (pilgrim) when he stones the Jamrah.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، وَعَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ قَالَ: كَانَ ابْنُ عَبَّاسٍ يُلَبِّي فِي الْعُمْرَةِ حَتَّى يَسْتَلِمَ الْحَجَرَ. وَكَانَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ يَقَعُ إِذَا دَخَلَ الْحَرَمَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ḥafṣ → Ḥajjāj and ‘Abd al-Malik → ‘Aṭā’
english.text: ‘Aṭā’ said: Ibn ‘Abbās used to recite Talbiyah in ‘Umrah until he touched the Stone. And Ibn ‘Umar used to stop when he entered the Sanctuary (Haram).

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي نَجِيحٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: حَتَّى يَسْتَلِمَ الْحَجَرَ وَقَالَ عَطَاءٌ: يَقْطَعُ إِذَا دَخَلَ الْقَرْيَةَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Ibn Abī Najīḥ → ‘Aṭā’ → Ibn ‘Abbās
english.text: Ibn ‘Abbās said: “(He continues) until he touches the Stone.” ‘Aṭā’ said: “He stops when he enters the town (Mecca).”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ أَبِي عَرُوبَةَ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، وَمُجَاهِدٍ، أَنَّهُمَا كَانَا إِذَا أَهَلَّا بِعُمْرَةٍ لَمْ يُمْسِكَا عَنِ التَّلْبِيَةِ حَتَّى يَسْتَلِمَانِ الْحَجَرَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ghundar → Sa‘īd ibn Abī ‘Arūbah → Qatādah → Sa‘īd ibn Jubayr and Mujāhid
english.text: Sa‘īd ibn Jubayr and Mujāhid, when they assumed Ihram for ‘Umrah, would not stop Talbiyah until they touched the Stone.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنِي عَبْدُ الْحَمِيدِ بْنُ جُبَيْرِ بْنِ شَيْبَةَ قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ عُمَرَ بْنَ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ، وَأَبَانَ بْنَ عُثْمَانَ يُلَبِّيَانِ بِذِي طُوًى فِي الْعُمْرَةِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Yaḥyā ibn Sa‘īd → Ibn Jurayj → ‘Abd al-Ḥamīd ibn Jubayr ibn Shaybah → ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Azīz and Abān ibn ‘Uthmān
english.text: ‘Abd al-Ḥamīd ibn Jubayr ibn Shaybah said: “I saw ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Azīz and Abān ibn ‘Uthmān reciting Talbiyah at Dhū Ṭuwā during ‘Umrah.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ قَالَ: يَقْطَعُ إِذَا دَخَلَ الْحَرَمَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Jarīr → Mughīrah → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Ibrāhīm said: “He stops when he enters the Sanctuary (Haram).”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ أَفْلَحَ، عَنِ الْقَاسِمِ قَالَ: يَقْطَعُ إِذَا رَأَى عُرُوشَ مَكَّةَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Wakī‘ → Aflaḥ → Al-Qāsim
english.text: Al-Qāsim said: “He stops when he sees the buildings of Mecca.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي نَجِيحٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ قَالَ: لَا يَقْطَعُ الْمُعْتَمِرُ حَتَّى يَسْتَلِمَ الْحَجَرَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Wakī‘ → Ibn Abī Najīḥ → Mujāhid
english.text: Mujāhid said: “The Mu'tamir does not stop until he touches the Stone.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ نُمَيْرٍ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ قَالَ: كَانَ يَقْطَعُ التَّلْبِيَةَ فِي الْعُمْرَةِ إِذَا دَخَلَ الْحَرَمَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Numayr → Hishām → His Father
english.text: Hishām's father used to stop Talbiyah in ‘Umrah when he entered the Sanctuary (Haram).

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَاتِمُ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ حَرْمَلَةَ، قَالَ سَعِيدُ بْنُ الْمُسَيِّبِ: الْإِهْلَالُ فِي الْعُمْرَةِ حَتَّى يَنْظُرَ إِلَى عُرُوشِ مَكَّةَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ḥātim ibn Ismā‘īl → ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn Ḥarmalah → Sa‘īd ibn al-Musayyib
english.text: Sa‘īd ibn al-Musayyib said: “Ihlal in ‘Umrah continues until one sees the buildings of Mecca.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَسْبَاطُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ قَالَ: كَانَ أَصْحَابُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ يُلَبِّيُونَ فِي الْعُمْرَةِ حَتَّى يَسْتَلِمُونَ الْحَجَرَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Asbāṭ ibn Muḥammad → Ash‘ath → Al-Ḥakam → Companions of ‘Abd Allāh
english.text: Al-Ḥakam said: “The companions of ‘Abd Allāh used to recite Talbiyah in ‘Umrah until they touched the Stone.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مُبَارَكٍ، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ طَاوُسٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ قَالَ: يَقْطَعُ فِي الْعُمْرَةِ إِذَا اسْتَلَمَ الْحَجَرَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Mubārak → Ma‘mar → Ibn Ṭāwūs → His Father
english.text: Ṭāwūs said: “He stops in ‘Umrah when he touches the Stone.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ قَالَ: "أَفَضْتُ مَعَ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، فَرَمَى سَبْعَ حَصَيَاتٍ اسْتَبْطَنَ الْوَادِيَ حَتَّى إِذَا فَرَغَ قَالَ: اللَّهُمَّ اجْعَلْهُ حَجًّا مَبْرُورًا، وَذَنْبًا مَغْفُورًا ثُمَّ قَالَ: هَكَذَا رَأَيْتُ الَّذِي أُنْزِلَ عَلَيْهِ سُورَةُ الْبَقَرَةِ صَنَعَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Idrīs → Layth → Mujāhid → Muḥammad ibn ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn Yazīd → His Father → ‘Abd Allāh
english.text: ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn Yazīd said: I departed with ‘Abd Allāh (Ibn Mas‘ūd). He stoned seven pebbles standing in the bottom of the valley. When he finished, he said: “O Allah, make it an accepted Hajj and a forgiven sin.” Then he said: “This is how I saw the One upon whom Surah Al-Baqarah was revealed (i.e., the Prophet ﷺ) doing it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الْأَحْوَصِ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنِ الْهَيْثَمِ بْنِ حَنَشٍ قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ ابْنَ عُمَرَ حِينَ رَمَى الْجِمَارَ يَقُولُ: اللَّهُمَّ اجْعَلْهُ حَجًّا مَبْرُورًا، وَذَنْبًا مَغْفُورًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu al-Aḥwaṣ → Abu Isḥāq → Al-Haytham ibn Ḥanash → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Al-Haytham ibn Ḥanash said: “I heard Ibn ‘Umar, when he stoned the Jamarat, saying: 'O Allah, make it an accepted Hajj and a forgiven sin.'”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ قَالَ: لَيْسَ عَلَى الْوُقُوفِ عِنْدَ الْجَمْرَتَيْنِ دُعَاءٌ مُؤَقَّتٌ، فَادْعُ بِمَا شِئْتَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ḥafṣ → Al-A‘mash
english.text: Al-A‘mash said: “There is no fixed supplication for standing at the two Jamrahs; invoke with whatever you wish.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ أَبِي عَدِيٍّ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ قَالَ: كَانَ الْحَسَنُ يَقُولُ: يَدْعُو عِنْدَ الْجِمَارِ لَهَا، وَلَا يُؤَقِّتُ شَيْئًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Abī ‘Adī → Ash‘ath → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Al-Ḥasan used to say: “He supplicates at the Jamarat for them (the rituals/himself), and he does not fix anything (specific supplication).”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِإِبْرَاهِيمَ: مَا أَقُولُ إِذَا رَمَيْتُ الْجَمْرَةَ؟ قُلِ: اللَّهُمَّ اجْعَلْهُ حَجًّا مَبْرُورًا، وَذَنْبًا مَغْفُورًا قَالَ: قُلْتُ: أَقُولُهُ مَعَ كُلِّ حَصَاةٍ؟ قَالَ: نَعَمْ إِنْ شِئْتَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Mahdī → Sufyān → Mughīrah → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Mughīrah said: I asked Ibrāhīm: “What should I say when I stone the Jamrah?” He said: “Say: 'O Allah, make it an accepted Hajj and a forgiven sin.'” I asked: “Do I say it with every pebble?” He said: “Yes, if you wish.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو خَالِدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِعَطَاءٍ: فِي الْجَمْرَةِ شَيْءٌ مُؤَقَّتٌ لَا يُزَادُ عَلَيْهِ؟ قَالَ: لَا قَوْلَ إِلَّا قَوْلُ جَابِرٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Khālid → Ibn Jurayj → ‘Aṭā’
english.text: Ibn Jurayj said: I asked ‘Aṭā’: “Is there a fixed thing (supplication) at the Jamrah that cannot be added to?” He said: “No saying except the saying of Jābir.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ عَيَّاشٍ، عَنْ أَبِي حَصِينٍ قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ سَعِيدَ بْنَ جُبَيْرٍ وَرَجُلًا مِنْ قُرَيْشٍ بَعْدَمَا أَفَاضَ الْإِمَامُ عَشِيَّةَ عَرَفَةَ، فَقَامَ سَعِيدُ بْنُ جُبَيْرٍ وَأَذَّنَ، وَأَمَّ الْقُرَشِيَّ بَعْدَمَا أَفَاضَ الْإِمَامُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Bakr ibn ‘Ayyāsh → Abu Ḥaṣīn → Sa‘īd ibn Jubayr
english.text: Abu Ḥaṣīn said: “I saw Sa‘īd ibn Jubayr and a man from Quraysh after the Imam had departed on the evening of ‘Arafah. Sa‘īd ibn Jubayr stood up, called Adhan, and led the Qurashi in prayer after the Imam had departed.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي شَرْقِيٍّ، عَنْ أَبِي عُثْمَانَ النَّهْدِيِّ، أَنَّهُ صَلَّى مَعَ عُمَرَ سَنَتَيْنِ الْمَغْرِبَ دُونَ جَمْعٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Wakī‘ → Shu‘bah → Abu Sharqī → Abu ‘Uthmān al-Nahdī → ‘Umar
english.text: Abu ‘Uthmān al-Nahdī reported that he prayed Maghrib with ‘Umar for two years before reaching Jam‘ (Muzdalifah).

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ حَسَنِ بْنِ صَالِحٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ أَنَّهُ صَلَّى دُونَ جَمْعٍ بِالْأَجْبَالِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Wakī‘ → Ḥasan ibn Ṣāliḥ → ‘Abd al-A‘lā → Sa‘īd ibn Jubayr → Ibn ‘Abbās
english.text: Ibn ‘Abbās prayed (Maghrib) before reaching Jam‘, at Al-Ajbāl (the mountains/passes).

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ أَبِي الزُّبَيْرِ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ قَالَ: لَا صَلَاةَ إِلَّا بِجَمْعٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Yaḥyā ibn Sa‘īd → Ibn Jurayj → Abu al-Zubayr → Jābir
english.text: Jābir said: “There is no prayer (Maghrib/Isha) except at Jam‘.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ خَالِدِ بْنِ أَبِي عُثْمَانَ قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ أَبَانَ بْنَ عُثْمَانَ صَلَّى الْمَغْرِبَ فِي الشِّعْبِ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَأْتِيَ جَمْعًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Mahdi → Khalid ibn Abi ‘Uthman → Aban ibn ‘Uthman
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Mahdi narrated to us, from Khalid ibn Abi ‘Uthman, who said: "I saw Aban ibn ‘Uthman pray Maghrib in the mountain pass before arriving at Jam‘ (Muzdalifah)."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُعَاذُ بْنُ مُعَاذٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَوْنٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدٍ قَالَ: لَا أَعْلَمُ الصَّلَاةَ لَيْلَةَ جَمْعٍ إِلَّا بِجَمْعٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Mu‘adh ibn Mu‘adh → Ibn ‘Awn → Muhammad
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Mu‘adh ibn Mu‘adh narrated to us, from Ibn ‘Awn, from Muhammad [Ibn Sirin], who said: "I do not know of the prayer on the night of Jam‘ (Muzdalifah) except at Jam‘."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنِ السَّكَنِ بْنِ الْمُغِيرَةِ قَالَ: صَلَّى بِنَا سَالِمٌ الْمَغْرِبَ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَأْتِيَ جَمْعًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Mahdi → As-Sakan ibn Al-Mughirah → Salim
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Mahdi narrated to us, from As-Sakan ibn Al-Mughirah, who said: "Salim prayed Maghrib with us before arriving at Jam‘."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الْأَحْوَصُ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ قَالَ: لَا يُصَلَّى الْمَغْرِبُ إِلَّا بِجَمْعٍ إِلَّا أَنْ تُخْطِيَ طَرِيقَكَ أَوْ تَضِلَّ رَاحِلَتُكَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Al-Ahwas → Layth → Mujahid
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Abu Al-Ahwas narrated to us, from Layth, from Mujahid, who said: "Maghrib is not to be prayed except at Jam‘, unless you miss your way or your mount goes astray."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ قَالَ: أَرَأَيْتَ إِنْ صَلَّاهَا فِي الطَّرِيقِ؟ قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ قَالَ: قُلْتُ: أَرَأَيْتَ إِنْ صَلَّى الْمَغْرِبَ فِي الطَّرِيقِ، وَالْعِشَاءَ بِجَمْعٍ؟ قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Idris → Ibn Jurayj → ‘Ata’
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Idris narrated to us, from Ibn Jurayj, from ‘Ata’, who said: "What do you think if he prayed it on the road?" He said: "There is no harm." He said: I said: "What do you think if he prayed Maghrib on the road and ‘Isha’ at Jam‘?" He said: "There is no harm."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ عَيَّاشٍ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ سَعِيدٍ قَالَ: كَانَ عُمَرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ وَاقِفًا بِعَرَفَةَ، فَقَالَ: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ، قَدْ جِئْتُمْ مِنَ الْقَرِيبِ وَالْبَعِيدِ، وَإِنَّكُمْ وَفْدٌ غَيْرُ وَاحِدٍ، وَإِنَّ السَّابِقَ لَيْسَ الَّذِي تَسْبِقُ دَابَّتُهُ وَلَا بَعِيرُهُ، وَإِنَّ السَّابِقَ مَنْ غَفَرَ اللَّهُ لَهُ ذَنْبَهُ، فَنَادَاهُ رَجُلٌ: أَيْنَ أُصَلِّي الْمَغْرِبَ؟ قَالَ: أَيْنَ أَدْرَكْتَ مِنْ وَادِيكَ هَذَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Bakr ibn ‘Ayyash → Yahya ibn Sa‘id → ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Aziz
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Abu Bakr ibn ‘Ayyash narrated to us, from Yahya ibn Sa‘id, who said: ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Aziz was standing at ‘Arafah, and he said: "O people, you have come from near and far, and you are not just one delegation. The winner is not the one whose beast or camel arrives first, but the winner is the one whose sin Allah forgives." A man called out to him: "Where should I pray Maghrib?" He said: "Wherever you are when [the time] overtakes you in this valley of yours."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَائِذُ بْنُ حَبِيبٍ، عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ عُرْوَةَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، أَنَّهُ إِذَا أَفَاضَ مِنْ عَرَفَاتٍ رُبَّمَا صَلَّى فِي الشِّعْبِ الْأَيْسَرِ عَلَى الْجَبَلِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → ‘A’idh ibn Habib → Hisham ibn ‘Urwah → His father (‘Urwah)
english.text: Abu Bakr said: ‘A’idh ibn Habib narrated to us, from Hisham ibn ‘Urwah, from his father, that "when he departed from Arafat, he would sometimes pray in the left pass on the mountain."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ أَبِي عَدِيٍّ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ: كَانَ يَكْرَهُ أَنْ يُصَلِّيَ دُونَ جَمْعٍ، فَإِنْ فَعَلَ أَجْزَأَ عَنْهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Abi ‘Adi → Ash‘ath → Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Abi ‘Adi narrated to us, from Ash‘ath, from Al-Hasan, who said: "He used to dislike praying before reaching Jam‘, but if he did so, it was sufficient for him."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ زَمْعَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ طَاوُسٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَكْرَهُ الصَّلَاةَ دُونَ الْمُزْدَلِفَةِ إِلَّا مِنْ ضَرُورَةٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Mahdi → Zam‘ah → Ibn Tawus → His father (Tawus)
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Mahdi narrated to us, from Zam‘ah, from Ibn Tawus, from his father, that "he used to dislike praying before Muzdalifah except out of necessity."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مُبَارَكٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ عُقْبَةَ، عَنْ كُرَيْبٍ قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنِي أُسَامَةُ بْنُ زَيْدٍ قَالَ: أَفَضْتُ مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ مِنْ عَرَفَاتٍ، فَلَمَّا كَانَ بِبَعْضِ الطَّرِيقِ قُلْتُ: الصَّلَاةُ، فَقَالَ: الصَّلَاةُ أَمَامَكَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Mubarak → Ibrahim ibn ‘Uqbah → Kurayb → Usamah ibn Zayd
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Mubarak narrated to us, from Ibrahim ibn ‘Uqbah, from Kurayb, who said: Usamah ibn Zayd told me, saying: "I departed with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ from Arafat. When we were partly along the way, I said: 'The prayer.' He said: 'The prayer is ahead of you.'"

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنِ الْأَسْوَدِ، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، أَنَّهُ صَلَّاهُمَا بِجَمْعٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Jarir → Mughirah → Ibrahim → Al-Aswad → Ma‘mar
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Jarir narrated to us, from Mughirah, from Ibrahim, from Al-Aswad, from Ma‘mar, that "he prayed both of them [Maghrib and Isha] at Jam‘."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي رَوَّادٍ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ إِذَا فَاتَتْهُ الصَّلَاةُ مَعَ الْإِمَامِ بِعَرَفَةَ جَمَعَ بَيْنَ الظُّهْرِ وَالْعَصْرِ فِي رَحْلِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hafs → Ibn Abi Rawwad → Nafi‘ → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Hafs narrated to us, from Ibn Abi Rawwad, from Nafi‘, from Ibn ‘Umar, "that if he missed the prayer with the Imam at ‘Arafah, he would combine Zuhr and ‘Asr in his dwelling."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ قَالَ: إِذَا صَلَّيْتَ فِي رَحْلِكَ بِعَرَفَةَ فَصَلِّ كُلَّ وَاحِدَةٍ مِنْهُمَا لِوَقْتِهَا، وَاجْعَلْ لِكُلِّ وَاحِدَةٍ مِنْهُمَا أَذَانًا وَإِقَامَةً

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Jarir → Mughirah → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Jarir narrated to us, from Mughirah, from Ibrahim, who said: "If you pray in your dwelling at ‘Arafah, pray each one of them at its time, and make an Adhan and Iqamah for each one of them."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ قَالَ: إِذَا صَلَّيْتَ فِي رَحْلِكَ فَإِنْ شِئْتَ فَاجْمَعْ بَيْنَهُمَا، وَإِنْ شِئْتَ فَصَلِّ كُلَّ وَاحِدَةٍ مِنْهُمَا لِوَقْتِهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Jarir → Layth → ‘Ata’
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Jarir narrated to us, from Layth, from ‘Ata’, who said: "If you pray in your dwelling, then combine them if you wish, and if you wish, pray each one of them at its time."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو خَالِدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ طَاوُسٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ قَالَ: مَا صَلَّى أَبِي قَطُّ مَعَ الْإِمَامِ بِعَرَفَةَ، وَكَانَ يَجْمَعُ بَيْنَهُمَا، وَكَانَ يَتَطَوَّعُ بَيْنَهُمَا، وَكَانَ يَفْعَلُ ذَلِكَ مِنَ الْحَيِّ حَتَّى يَأْتِيَ مَكَّةَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Khalid → Ibn Jurayj → Ibn Tawus → His father (Tawus)
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Abu Khalid narrated to us, from Ibn Jurayj, from Ibn Tawus, from his father, who said: "My father never prayed with the Imam at ‘Arafah. He used to combine them, and he would perform voluntary prayers between them. He used to do this from the time he left the neighborhood until he arrived in Makkah."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ السَّلَامِ بْنُ حَرْبٍ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ قَالَ: يُصَلِّي كُلَّ صَلَاةٍ لِوَقْتِهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → ‘Abd as-Salam ibn Harb → Mughirah → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr said: ‘Abd as-Salam ibn Harb narrated to us, from Mughirah, from Ibrahim, who said: "He prays every prayer at its time."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ عَدِيِّ بْنِ ثَابِتٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ، عَنْ أَبِي أَيُّوبَ، أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ جَمَعَ بَيْنَ الْمَغْرِبَ وَالْعِشَاءِ بِالْمُزْدَلِفَةِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki‘ → Shu‘bah → ‘Adi ibn Thabit → ‘Abdullah ibn Yazid → Abu Ayyub
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki‘ narrated to us, from Shu‘bah, from ‘Adi ibn Thabit, from ‘Abdullah ibn Yazid, from Abu Ayyub, "that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ combined Maghrib and ‘Isha’ at Muzdalifah."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ سَلَمَةَ بْنِ كُهَيْلٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ أَنَّهُ جَمَعَ بَيْنَ الصَّلَاتَيْنِ بِجَمْعٍ ثُمَّ قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فَعَلَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Sharik → Salamah ibn Kuhayl → Sa‘id ibn Jubayr → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Sharik narrated to us, from Salamah ibn Kuhayl, from Sa‘id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn ‘Umar, that he combined the two prayers at Jam‘, then said: "I saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ do it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سَلَّامٌ أَبُو الْأَحْوَصِ، عَنْ سِمَاكٍ، عَنِ النُّعْمَانِ بْنِ حُمَيْدٍ قَالَ: رَأَيْتُ عُمَرَ بْنَ الْخَطَّابِ جَمَعَ الْمَغْرِبَ وَالْعِشَاءَ بِجَمْعٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Salam Abu Al-Ahwas → Simak → An-Nu‘man ibn Humayd → ‘Umar ibn Al-Khattab
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Salam Abu Al-Ahwas narrated to us, from Simak, from An-Nu‘man ibn Humayd, who said: I saw ‘Umar ibn Al-Khattab combine Maghrib and ‘Isha’ at Jam‘.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ عَيَّاشٍ، وَأَبُو الْأَحْوَصِ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ قَالَ: صَلَّيْتُ مَعَ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْمَغْرِبَ بِجَمْعٍ بِأَذَانٍ وَإِقَامَةٍ، ثُمَّ أُتِينَا بِعِشَاءٍ فَتَعَشَّيْنَا، ثُمَّ صَلَّى بِنَا الْعِشَاءَ بِأَذَانٍ وَإِقَامَةٍ زَادَ فِيهِ أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ عَيَّاشٍ، قَالَ أَبُو إِسْحَاقَ: فَلَقِيتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ فَأَخْبَرْتُهُ، فَقَالَ: وَكَذَلِكَ يَفْعَلُ أَهْلُ الْبَيْتِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Bakr ibn ‘Ayyash & Abu Al-Ahwas → Abu Ishaq → ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn Yazid → ‘Abdullah (Ibn Mas‘ud)
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Abu Bakr ibn ‘Ayyash and Abu Al-Ahwas narrated to us, from Abu Ishaq, from ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn Yazid, who said: "I prayed Maghrib with ‘Abdullah [ibn Mas‘ud] at Jam‘ with an Adhan and Iqamah. Then dinner was brought to us and we ate dinner. Then he prayed ‘Isha’ with us with an Adhan and Iqamah." Abu Bakr ibn ‘Ayyash added in it: Abu Ishaq said: I met Abu Ja‘far and informed him, and he said: "And that is how the People of the House (Ahl al-Bayt) do."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ أَبِي عَدِيٍّ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، وَمُحَمَّدٍ قَالَ: مِنَ السُّنَّةِ أَنْ يُجْمَعَ بَيْنَهُمَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Abi ‘Adi → Hisham → Al-Hasan & Muhammad
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Abi ‘Adi narrated to us, from Hisham, from Al-Hasan and Muhammad, who said: "It is from the Sunnah to combine them."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَائِذُ بْنُ حَبِيبٍ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ إِذَا أَفَاضَ مِنْ عَرَفَاتٍ إِنَّمَا يُصَلِّي فِي الشِّعْبِ الْأَيْسَرِ عَلَى الْجَبَلِ، وأَنَّهُ كَانَ يَجْمَعُ بَيْنَ الْمَغْرِبِ وَالْعِشَاءِ الْآخِرَةِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → ‘A’idh ibn Habib → Hisham → His father (‘Urwah)
english.text: Abu Bakr said: ‘A’idh ibn Habib narrated to us, from Hisham, from his father, "that when he departed from Arafat, he would only pray in the left pass on the mountain, and that he used to combine Maghrib and the final ‘Isha’."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا الْفَضْلُ بْنُ دُكَيْنٍ، عَنْ حُسَيْنِ بْنِ عَقِيلٍ، عَنِ الضَّحَّاكِ قَالَ: يُجْمَعُ بَيْنَهُمَا بِجَمْعٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Al-Fadl ibn Dukayn → Husayn ibn ‘Aqil → Ad-Dahhak
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Al-Fadl ibn Dukayn narrated to us, from Husayn ibn ‘Aqil, from Ad-Dahhak, who said: "They are combined at Jam‘."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ شَرِيكٍ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ، أَنَّ عَلِيًّا جَمَعَ بَيْنَهُمَا بِجَمْعٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki‘ → Sharik → Abu Ishaq → Abu Ja‘far → ‘Ali
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki‘ narrated to us, from Sharik, from Abu Ishaq, from Abu Ja‘far, that ‘Ali "combined them at Jam‘."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَاتِمُ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنْ جَعْفَرٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ جَابِرِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ: صَلَّى رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ الْمَغْرِبَ وَالْعِشَاءَ بِأَذَانٍ وَاحِدٍ وَإِقَامَتَيْنِ، وَلَمْ يُسَبِّحْ بَيْنَهُمَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hatim ibn Isma‘il → Ja‘far → His father (Muhammad al-Baqir) → Jabir ibn ‘Abdullah
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Hatim ibn Isma‘il narrated to us, from Ja‘far, from his father, from Jabir ibn ‘Abdullah, who said: "The Messenger of Allah ﷺ prayed Maghrib and ‘Isha’ with one Adhan and two Iqamahs, and he did not glorify Allah (perform Tasbih/voluntary prayer) between them."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مُسْهِرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنْ عَدِيِّ بْنِ ثَابِتٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ، عَنْ أَبِي أَيُّوبَ قَالَ: صَلَّى رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ بِالْمُزْدَلِفَةِ الْمَغْرِبَ وَالْعِشَاءَ بِإِقَامَةٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Mushir → Ibn Abi Layla → ‘Adi ibn Thabit → ‘Abdullah ibn Yazid → Abu Ayyub
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Mushir narrated to us, from Ibn Abi Layla, from ‘Adi ibn Thabit, from ‘Abdullah ibn Yazid, from Abu Ayyub, who said: "The Messenger of Allah ﷺ prayed Maghrib and ‘Isha’ at Muzdalifah with an Iqamah."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ نُمَيْرٍ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ أَبِي خَالِدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ قَالَ: قَالَ سَعِيدُ بْنُ جُبَيْرٍ: أَفَضْنَا مَعَ ابْنِ عُمَرَ حَتَّى أَتَيْنَا جَمْعًا فَصَلَّى بِنَا الْمَغْرِبَ وَالْعِشَاءَ بِإِقَامَةٍ وَاحِدَةٍ، ثُمَّ انْصَرَفَ فَقَالَ: صَلَّى بِنَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فِي هَذَا الْمَكَانِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Numayr → Isma‘il ibn Abi Khalid → Abu Ishaq → Sa‘id ibn Jubayr → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Numayr narrated to us, from Isma‘il ibn Abi Khalid, from Abu Ishaq, who said: Sa‘id ibn Jubayr said: We departed with Ibn ‘Umar until we came to Jam‘, and he led us in praying Maghrib and ‘Isha’ with one Iqamah. Then he turned and said: "The Messenger of Allah ﷺ prayed with us in this place."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ نُمَيْرٍ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ قَالَ: صَلَّيْتُ مَعَهُ الْمَغْرِبَ وَالْعِشَاءَ بِإِقَامَةٍ وَاحِدَةٍ، وَقَالَ: فَعَلْتُ مَعَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Numayr → Al-Hakam → Sa‘id ibn Jubayr → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Numayr narrated to us, from Al-Hakam, from Sa‘id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn ‘Umar, who said: "I prayed Maghrib and ‘Isha’ with him with one Iqamah, and he said: 'I did it with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ.'"

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الْأَحْوَصِ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ قَالَ: اتَّفَقَ عَلِيٌّ، وَعَبْدُ اللَّهِ أَنَّ كُلَّ صَلَاةٍ تُجْمَعُ بِأَذَانٍ وَإِقَامَةٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Al-Ahwas → Abu Ishaq → Abu Ja‘far → ‘Ali & ‘Abdullah
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Abu Al-Ahwas narrated to us, from Abu Ishaq, from Abu Ja‘far, who said: ‘Ali and ‘Abdullah [Ibn Mas‘ud] agreed "that every prayer is gathered with an Adhan and an Iqamah."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي إِسْمَاعِيلَ قَالَ: صَلَّيْتُ بِجَمْعٍ مَعَ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ الْمَغْرِبَ وَالْعِشَاءَ بِإِقَامَةٍ وَاحِدَةٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Fudayl → Muhammad ibn Abi Isma‘il → Sa‘id ibn Jubayr
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Fudayl narrated to us, from Muhammad ibn Abi Isma‘il, who said: "I prayed Maghrib and ‘Isha’ at Jam‘ with Sa‘id ibn Jubayr with one Iqamah."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ، عَنْ أَنَسِ بْنِ سِيرِينَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، أَنَّهُ صَلَّى الصَّلَاتَيْنِ بِجَمْعٍ بِإِقَامَةٍ وَاحِدَةٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Mu‘awiyah → ‘Abdullah ibn ‘Umar → Anas ibn Sirin → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Abu Mu‘awiyah narrated to us, from ‘Abdullah ibn ‘Umar, from Anas ibn Sirin, from Ibn ‘Umar, "that he prayed both prayers at Jam‘ with one Iqamah."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، أَنَّ الْأَسْوَدَ أَقَامَ الصَّلَاةَ وَصَلَّى الْمَغْرِبَ بِالْمُزْدَلِفَةِ، ثُمَّ تَعَشَّى وَصَلَّى الْعِشَاءَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki‘ → Sufyan → Mansur → Ibrahim → Al-Aswad
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki‘ narrated to us, from Sufyan, from Mansur, from Ibrahim, "that Al-Aswad established the prayer (made Iqamah) and prayed Maghrib at Muzdalifah, then he ate dinner, and [then] prayed ‘Isha’."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا الْفَضْلُ بْنُ دُكَيْنٍ، عَنْ مِسْعَرٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْكَرِيمِ قَالَ: صَلَّيْتُ خَلْفَ سَالِمٍ الْمَغْرِبَ وَالْعِشَاءَ بِجَمْعٍ بِأَذَانٍ وَاحِدٍ وَإِقَامَتَيْنِ، فَلَقِيتُ نَافِعًا فَقُلْتُ لَهُ: هَكَذَا كَانَ يَصْنَعُ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ؟ قَالَ: هَكَذَا، فَلَقِيتُ عَطَاءً فَقُلْتُ: "قَدْ كُنْتُ أَقُولُ لَهُمْ: لَا صَلَاةَ إِلَّا بِإِقَامَةٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Al-Fadl ibn Dukayn → Mis‘ar → ‘Abd al-Karim → Salim / Nafi‘ / ‘Ata’
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Al-Fadl ibn Dukayn narrated to us, from Mis‘ar, from ‘Abd al-Karim, who said: "I prayed Maghrib and ‘Isha’ behind Salim at Jam‘ with one Adhan and two Iqamahs." I met Nafi‘ and said to him: "Is this how ‘Abdullah used to do?" He said: "Like this." I met ‘Ata’ and said: "I used to tell them: There is no prayer without an Iqamah."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ 989 إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ أَبِي خَالِدٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ قَالَ: عَلَيْهِ هَدْيٌ آخَرُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Isma‘il ibn Abi Khalid → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Hushaym narrated to us, from Isma‘il ibn Abi Khalid, from Ibrahim, who said: "He owes another sacrificial animal (Hady)."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ قَالَ: عَلَيْهِ هَدْيٌ آخَرُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Hajjaj → ‘Ata’
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Hushaym narrated to us, from Hajjaj, from ‘Ata’, who said: "He owes another sacrificial animal."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ ذَرٍّ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ قَالَ: إِذَا حَلَقَ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَذْبَحَ هَدْيَهُ قَالَ: عَلَيْهِ هَدْيٌ آخَرُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki‘ → ‘Umar ibn Dharr → Mujahid
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki‘ narrated to us, from ‘Umar ibn Dharr, from Mujahid, who said: "If he shaves before his sacrificial animal is slaughtered," he said: "He owes another sacrificial animal."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ حُمَيْدٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَقُولُ ذَلِكَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hushaym → Humayd → Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Hushaym narrated to us, from Humayd, from Al-Hasan, that he used to say that.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، عَنْ عَلْقَمَةَ قَالَ: عَلَيْهِ دَمٌ قَالَ الْأَعْمَشُ: فَذَكَرْتُ ذَلِكَ لِإِبْرَاهِيمَ فَقَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي سَعِيدُ بْنُ جُبَيْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ بِمِثْلِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Mu‘awiyah → Al-A‘mash → Ibrahim → ‘Alqamah
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Abu Mu‘awiyah narrated to us, from Al-A‘mash, from Ibrahim, from ‘Alqamah, who said: "He owes a sacrifice (Dam/Blood)." Al-A‘mash said: I mentioned that to Ibrahim, and he said: Sa‘id ibn Jubayr narrated to me, from Ibn ‘Abbas, something similar.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ أَبِي خَالِدٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ قَالَ: يَذْبَحُ شَاةً أَوْ يُطْعِمُ سِتَّةَ مَسَاكِينَ أَوْ يَصُومُ ثَلَاثَةَ أَيَّامٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki‘ → Isma‘il ibn Abi Khalid → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki‘ narrated to us, from Isma‘il ibn Abi Khalid, from Ibrahim, who said: "He slaughters a sheep, or feeds six poor people, or fasts for three days."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ صَدَقَةَ بْنِ يَسَارٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ قَالَ: وَقَّتَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ لِأَهْلِ الْمَدِينَةِ ذَا الْحُلَيْفَةِ، وَلِأَهْلِ الشَّامِ الْجُحْفَةَ، وَلِأَهْلِ الْيَمَنِ يَلَمْلَمَ، وَلِأَهْلِ نَجْدٍ قَرْنًا فَقَالَ رَجُلٌ: فَلِأَهْلِ الْعِرَاقِ قَالَ: لَا عِرَاقَ يَوْمَئِذٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Jarir → Sadaqah ibn Yasar → ‘Abdullah ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Jarir narrated to us, from Sadaqah ibn Yasar, from ‘Abdullah ibn ‘Umar, who said: "The Messenger of Allah ﷺ designated Dhu Al-Hulayfah for the people of Medina, Al-Juhfah for the people of Ash-Sham, Yalamlam for the people of Yemen, and Qarn for the people of Najd." A man said: "What about the people of Iraq?" He said: "There was no Iraq (Islamic presence) at that time."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ قَالَ: جَاءَ رَجُلٌ فَقَالَ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ مِنْ أَيْنَ يُهَلُّ؟ قَالَ: يُهِلُّ أَهْلُ الْمَدِينَةِ مِنْ ذِي الْحُلَيْفَةِ، وَأَهْلُ الشَّامِ مِنَ الْجُحْفَةِ، وَأَهْلُ نَجْدٍ مِنْ قَرْنٍ فَقَالَ ابْنُ عُمَرَ: وَيَقُولُونَ: وَأَهْلُ الْيَمَنِ مِنْ يَلَمْلَمُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Ayyub → Nafi‘ → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn ‘Ulayyah narrated to us, from Ayyub, from Nafi‘, from Ibn ‘Umar, who said: A man came and said: "O Messenger of Allah, from where do we assume Ihram?" He said: "The people of Medina assume Ihram from Dhu Al-Hulayfah, the people of Ash-Sham from Al-Juhfah, and the people of Najd from Qarn." Ibn ‘Umar said: And they say: "And the people of Yemen from Yalamlam."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ نُمَيْرٍ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ قَالَ: وَقَّتَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ لِأَهْلِ الْمَدِينَةِ ذَا الْحُلَيْفَةِ، وَلِأَهْلِ الشَّامِ الْجُحْفَةَ، وَلِأَهْلِ الْيَمَنِ يَلَمْلَمَ وَتِهَامَةَ، وَلِأَهْلِ نَجْدٍ قَرْنًا، وَلِأَهْلِ الْعِرَاقِ ذَاتَ عِرْقٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Numayr → Hajjaj → ‘Ata’ → Jabir
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Numayr narrated to us, from Hajjaj, from ‘Ata’, from Jabir, who said: "The Messenger of Allah ﷺ designated Dhu Al-Hulayfah for the people of Medina, Al-Juhfah for the people of Ash-Sham, Yalamlam and Tihamah for the people of Yemen, Qarn for the people of Najd, and Dhat ‘Irq for the people of Iraq."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وُهَيْبٌ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ طَاوُسٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ وَقَّتَ لِأَهْلِ الْمَدِينَةِ ذَا الْحُلَيْفَةِ، وَلِأَهْلِ الشَّامِ الْجُحْفَةَ، وَلِأَهْلِ نَجْدٍ قَرْنَ الْمَنَازِلِ، وَلِأَهْلِ الْيَمَنِ يَلَمْلَمُ وَقَالَ: هُنَّ لَهُمْ وَلِكُلِّ آتٍ عَلَيْهِنَّ مِنْ غَيْرِهِمْ مِمَّنْ أَرَادَ الْحَجَّ وَالْعُمْرَةَ، وَمَنْ كَانَ دُونَ ذَلِكَ فَمِنْ حَيْثُ أَنْشَأَ أَهْلُ مَكَّةَ مِنْ مَكَّةَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Yahya → Wuhib → ‘Abdullah ibn Tawus → His father → Ibn ‘Abbas
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Yahya narrated to us, saying: Wuhib narrated to us, saying: ‘Abdullah ibn Tawus narrated to us, from his father, from Ibn ‘Abbas, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ "designated Dhu Al-Hulayfah for the people of Medina, Al-Juhfah for the people of Ash-Sham, Qarn Al-Manazil for the people of Najd, and Yalamlam for the people of Yemen." And he said: "They are for them and for anyone who comes to them from others who intend Hajj and Umrah. And whoever is closer than that, then from where he starts; even the people of Makkah from Makkah."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ أَبِي زِيَادٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: وَقَّتَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ لِأَهْلِ الْمَشْرِقِ الْعَقِيقَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki‘ → Sufyan → Yazid ibn Abi Ziyad → Muhammad ibn ‘Ali → Ibn ‘Abbas
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki‘ narrated to us, from Sufyan, from Yazid ibn Abi Ziyad, from Muhammad ibn ‘Ali, from Ibn ‘Abbas, who said: "The Messenger of Allah ﷺ designated Al-‘Aqiq for the people of the East."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ قَالَ: وَقَّتَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ لِأَهْلِ الْعِرَاقِ ذَاتَ عِرْقٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Mu‘awiyah → Ibn Jurayj → ‘Ata’
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Abu Mu‘awiyah narrated to us, from Ibn Jurayj, from ‘Ata’, who said: "The Messenger of Allah ﷺ designated Dhat ‘Irq for the people of Iraq."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ بْنِ أَبِي كَثِيرٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لَهُ: مِنْ أَيْنَ يُهَلُّ؟ قَالَ: مِنَ الْبَيْدَاءِ، هَهُنَا أَهَلَّ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ لِحَجَّةٍ، وَمِنْهَا أَهَلَّ لِعُمْرَتِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Yahya ibn Sa‘id → ‘Abd al-Malik ibn Abi Kathir → Sa‘id ibn Al-Musayyib
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Yahya ibn Sa‘id narrated to us, from ‘Abd al-Malik ibn Abi Kathir, from Sa‘id ibn Al-Musayyib, who said: I said to him: "From where is Ihram assumed?" He said: "From Al-Bayda’. That is where the Messenger of Allah ﷺ assumed Ihram for Hajj, and from there he assumed Ihram for his Umrah."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، أَنَّ عُمَرَ، وَقَّتَ لِأَهْلِ الْعِرَاقِ ذَاتَ عِرْقٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Yahya ibn Sa‘id → ‘Ubayd Allah → Nafi‘ → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Yahya ibn Sa‘id narrated to us, from ‘Ubayd Allah, from Nafi‘, from Ibn ‘Umar, that ‘Umar "designated Dhat ‘Irq for the people of Iraq."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: قَالَ عُمَرُ لِأَهْلِ الْعِرَاقِ: انْظُرُوا حِذَاءَ قَرْنٍ فَوَجَدُوا حِذَاءَهَا ذَاتَ عِرْقٍ، وَقَرْنٌ أَقْرَبُ إِلَى مَكَّةَ مِنْ ذَاتِ عِرْقٍ قَالَ: فَجَعَلَهُ لِأَهْلِ الْعِرَاقِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Jarir → Layth → Nafi‘ → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Jarir narrated to us, from Layth, from Nafi‘, from Ibn ‘Umar, who said: ‘Umar said to the people of Iraq: "Look for what aligns with Qarn." They found Dhat ‘Irq aligning with it, and Qarn is closer to Makkah than Dhat ‘Irq. He said: So he appointed it for the people of Iraq.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ الْأَسْوَدِ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ لَا يَدَعُ أَحَدًا مِنْ أَهْلِهِ يُجَاوِزُ الْعَقِيقَ وَهُوَ غَيْرُ مُحْرِمٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki‘ → Sufyan → Jabir → ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn Al-Aswad → His father
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki‘ narrated to us, from Sufyan, from Jabir, from ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn Al-Aswad, from his father, "that he would not let anyone of his family cross Al-‘Aqiq while not in Ihram."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَوْنٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ سِيرِينَ قَالَ: "حَدَّ لِلنَّاسِ خَمْسَةً: لِأَهْلِ الْمَدِينَةِ ذَا الْحُلَيْفَةِ، وَلِأَهْلِ مَكَّةَ التَّنْعِيمَ، وَلِأَهْلِ الشَّامِ الْجُحْفَةَ، وَلِأَهْلِ الْيَمَنِ يَلَمْلَمَ، وَلِأَهْلِ نَجْدٍ قَرْنًا أَوْ قَالَ: لِأَهْلِ الْعِرَاقِ قَرْنٌ"، فَلَمَّا كَانَ بَعْدُ قَالُوا: لِابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ: لَيْسَ لَنَا طَرِيقٌ عَلَى قَرْنٍ قَالَ: إِزَاءَهُ ذَاتُ عِرْقٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Ibn ‘Awn → Ibn Sirin
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn ‘Ulayyah narrated to us, from Ibn ‘Awn, from Ibn Sirin, who said: "He set limits for the people in five places: for the people of Medina Dhu Al-Hulayfah, for the people of Makkah At-Tan‘im, for the people of Ash-Sham Al-Juhfah, for the people of Yemen Yalamlam, and for the people of Najd Qarn - or he said: for the people of Iraq Qarn." Later on, they said to Ibn ‘Abbas: "We do not have a route through Qarn." He said: "Opposite to it is Dhat ‘Irq."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ نُمَيْرٍ، عَنْ عُمَارَةَ بْنِ زَاذَانَ، عَنْ ثَابِتٍ، عَنْ أَنَسٍ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يُحْرِمُ مِنْ ذَاتِ عِرْقٍ، وَلَا يُكَلِّمُ أَحَدًا مِنَ النَّاسِ حَتَّى يَطُوفَ بِالْبَيْتِ إِلَّا مَا لَا بُدَّ مِنْهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Numayr → ‘Umarah ibn Zadhoan → Thabit → Anas
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Numayr narrated to us, from ‘Umarah ibn Zadhoan, from Thabit, from Anas, "that he used to assume Ihram from Dhat ‘Irq, and he would not speak to anyone among the people until he circumambulated the House, except what was necessary."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْرَائِيلَ، عَنْ ثَوْرٍ قَالَ: حَجَجْتُ مَعَ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، وَمُجَاهِدٍ فَأَحْرَمْنَا مِنَ الْعَقِيقِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki‘ → Isra’il → Thawr
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki‘ narrated to us, from Isra’il, from Thawr, who said: "I performed Hajj with Sa‘id ibn Jubayr and Mujahid, and we assumed Ihram from Al-‘Aqiq."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا الْفَضْلُ بْنُ دُكَيْنٍ، عَنْ إِسْرَائِيلَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ سُوَيْدِ بْنِ غَفَلَةَ، قَالَ: حَجَجْتُ مَعَ ابْنِ عُمَرَ فَأَحْرَمَ مِنْ ذَاتِ عِرْقٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Al-Fadl ibn Dukayn → Isra’il → Ibrahim ibn ‘Abd al-A‘la → Suwayd ibn Ghaflah → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Al-Fadl ibn Dukayn narrated to us, from Isra’il, from Ibrahim ibn ‘Abd al-A‘la, from Suwayd ibn Ghaflah, who said: "I performed Hajj with Ibn ‘Umar, and he assumed Ihram from Dhat ‘Irq."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ مَسْرُوقًا يَقُولُ: لِأَهْلِ الْعِرَاقِ الْعَقِيقُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki‘ → Sufyan → Abu Ishaq → Masruq
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki‘ narrated to us, from Sufyan, from Abu Ishaq, who said: I heard Masruq saying: "For the people of Iraq, it is Al-‘Aqiq."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ عَاصِمٍ، عَنْ أَنَسٍ قَالَ: "إِذَا خَرَجْتَ وَأَنْتَ تُرِيدُ الْحَجَّ فَلَا تَقُلْ: إِنِّي حَاجٌّ حَتَّى تُهِلَّ" قَالَ: فَقُلْتُ: أَيُّ شَيْءٍ أَقُولُ؟ قَالَ: قُلْ: إِنِّي مُسَافِرٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Fudayl → ‘Asim → Anas
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Fudayl narrated to us, from ‘Asim, from Anas, who said: "If you go out intending Hajj, do not say: 'I am a pilgrim (Hajj),' until you assume Ihram." He said: I said: "What should I say?" He said: "Say: 'I am a traveler.'"

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ، عَنْ خَيْثَمَةَ قَالَ: قَالَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ: "مَنْ أَرَادَ هَذَا الْوَجْهَ فَلَا يَقُلْ إِنِّي حَاجٌّ، إِنَّمَا الْحَاجُّ الْمُحْرِمُ: وَلْيَقُلْ إِنِّي وَافِدٌ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Fudayl → Sa‘id ibn Al-Musayyib → Khaythamah → ‘Abdullah
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Fudayl narrated to us, from Sa‘id ibn Al-Musayyib, from Khaythamah, who said: ‘Abdullah said: "Whoever intends this destination should not say 'I am a pilgrim' (Hajj). The pilgrim is only the Muhrim. Let him say: 'I am a visitor (Wafid).'"

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، فِي الرَّجُلِ يَحُجُّ فَيَبْدُو لَهُ أَنْ يَرْجِعَ قَبْلَ أَنْ يُحْرِمَ، قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ أَنْ يَرْجِعَ قَبْلَ أَنْ يُحْرِمَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Fudayl → Mughirah → Ibrahim
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Fudayl narrated to us, from Mughirah, from Ibrahim, regarding a man who intends Hajj then decides to return before assuming Ihram. He said: "There is no harm in him returning before assuming Ihram."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ بْنِ أَبِي غَنِيَّةَ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ قَالَ: إِذَا خَرَجَ الرَّجُلُ إِلَى مَكَّةَ، ثُمَّ بَدَا لَهُ أَنْ يَرْجِعَ رَجَعَ مَا لَمْ يُهِلَّ بِالْحَجِّ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Yahya ibn ‘Abd al-Malik ibn Abi Ghaniyah → His father → Al-Hakam
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Yahya ibn ‘Abd al-Malik ibn Abi Ghaniyah narrated to us, from his father, from Al-Hakam, who said: "If a man goes out to Makkah, then decides to return, he may return as long as he has not assumed Ihram for Hajj."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مُعْتَمِرُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، وَطَاوُسٍ، قَالَا: إِنْ شَاءَ رَجَعَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Mu‘tamir ibn Sulayman → Layth → ‘Ata’ & Tawus
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Mu‘tamir ibn Sulayman narrated to us, from Layth, from ‘Ata’ and Tawus, who both said: "If he wishes, he may return."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبَّادُ بْنُ الْعَوَّامِ، عَنْ هِلَالِ بْنِ خَبَّابٍ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، أَنَّ ضُبَاعَةَ ابْنَةَ الزُّبَيْرِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْمُطَّلِبِ، أَتَتِ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ، قَالَتْ: كَيْفَ أَقُولُ؟ قَالَ: "قُولِي: لَبَّيْكَ اللَّهُمَّ لَبَّيْكَ، مَحِلِّي مِنَ الْأَرْضِ حَيْثُ حَبَسْتَنِي

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → ‘Abbad ibn Al-‘Awwam → Hilal ibn Khabbab → ‘Ikrimah → Ibn ‘Abbas
english.text: Abu Bakr said: ‘Abbad ibn Al-‘Awwam narrated to us, from Hilal ibn Khabbab, from ‘Ikrimah, from Ibn ‘Abbas, that Duba‘ah bint Az-Zubayr ibn ‘Abd al-Muttalib came to the Prophet ﷺ and said: "How should I say [the Talbiyah]?" He said: "Say: 'Labbayk Allahumma Labbayk, my place of release is where You detain me.'"

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ قَالَ: كَانَ عَطَاءٌ يُلَبِّي وَلَيْسَ بِمُحْرِمٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hafs → Ibn Jurayj → ‘Ata’
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Hafs narrated to us, from Ibn Jurayj, who said: ‘Ata’ used to "recite Talbiyah while he was not a Muhrim."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، فِي الرَّجُلِ يُعَلِّمُ الرَّجُلَ التَّلْبِيَةَ وَهُوَ حَلَالٌ قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ بِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Mahdi → Shu‘bah → Al-Hakam
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Mahdi narrated to us, from Shu‘bah, from Al-Hakam, regarding a man teaching another man the Talbiyah while he is not in Ihram (Halal). He said: "There is no harm in it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ قَالَ: كَانَ أَهْلُونَا يُعَلِّمُونَا ذَلِكَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Mahdi → Shu‘bah → Ayyub → Mujahid
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Mahdi narrated to us, from Shu‘bah, from Ayyub, from Mujahid, who said: "Our families used to teach us that."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنِ الرَّبِيعِ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، وَعَطَاءٍ، قَالَا: كَانَا لَا يَرَيَانِ بِهِ بَأْسًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Mahdi → Ar-Rabi‘ → Al-Hasan & ‘Ata’
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Mahdi narrated to us, from Ar-Rabi‘, from Al-Hasan and ‘Ata’, who said: "They saw no harm in it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ مُسْهِرٍ، وَابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ أَبِي زِيَادٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ سَابِطٍ، عَنْ عَيَّاشِ بْنِ أَبِي رَبِيعَةَ الْمَخْزُومِيِّ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: لَا تَزَالُ هَذِهِ الْأُمَّةُ بِخَيْرٍ مَا عَظَّمُوا هَذِهِ الْحُرْمَةَ حَقَّ تَعْظِيمِهَا، فَإِذَا ضَيَّعُوا ذَلِكَ هَلَكُوا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → ‘Ali ibn Mushir & Ibn Fudayl → Yazid ibn Abi Ziyad → ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn Sabit → ‘Ayyash ibn Abi Rabi‘ah
english.text: Abu Bakr said: ‘Ali ibn Mushir and Ibn Fudayl narrated to us, from Yazid ibn Abi Ziyad, who said: ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn Sabit narrated to us, from ‘Ayyash ibn Abi Rabi‘ah Al-Makhzumi, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "This nation will continue to be in goodness as long as they venerate this sanctity [of Makkah] as it deserves to be venerated. If they waste that, they will perish."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: هَذِهِ حَرَمٌ، يَعْنِي مَكَّةَ، أَحَلَّهَا اللَّهُ لِي سَاعَةً مِنْ نَهَارٍ، لَا يُعْضَدُ شَوْكُهَا، وَلَا يُنَفَّرُ صَيْدُهَا، وَلَا يُخْتَلَى خَلَاهَا، وَلَا يُرْفَعُ لُقَطَتُهَا إِلَّا لِمُنْشِدٍ فَقَالَ الْعَبَّاسُ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ، إِنَّ أَهْلَ مَكَّةَ لَا صَبْرَ لَهُمْ عَنِ الْإِذْخِرِ لِقَيْنِهِمْ وَبُنْيَانِهِمْ، فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: إِلَّا الْإِذْخِرَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Fudayl → Yazid → Mujahid → Ibn ‘Abbas
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Fudayl narrated to us, from Yazid, from Mujahid, from Ibn ‘Abbas, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "This is a sanctuary - meaning Makkah. Allah made it lawful for me for an hour of a day. Its thorns are not to be cut, its game is not to be chased, its fresh grass is not to be cut, and its lost items are not to be picked up except by one who announces it." Al-‘Abbas said: "O Messenger of Allah, indeed the people of Makkah cannot do without Idhkhir (lemon grass) for their blacksmiths and their buildings." The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Except Idhkhir."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ مِسْعَرٍ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ مُرَّةَ، عَنْ طَلْقِ بْنِ حَبِيبٍ، قَالَ: قَالَ عُمَرُ: يَا أَهْلَ مَكَّةَ، اتَّقُوا اللَّهَ فِي حَرَمِ اللَّهِ، أَتَدْرُونَ مَنْ كَانَ سَاكِنَ هَذَا الْبَيْتِ؟ كَانَ بِهِ بَنُو فُلَانٍ، فَأَحَلُّوا حَرَمَهُ فَأُهْلِكُوا، وَكَانَ بِهِ بَنُو فُلَانٍ، فَأَحَلُّوا حَرَمَهُ فَأُهْلِكُوا، حَتَّى ذَكَرَ مَا شَاءَ اللَّهُ مِنْ قَبَائِلِ الْعَرَبِ أَنْ يَذْكُرَ ثُمَّ قَالَ: لَأَنْ أَعْمَلَ عَشْرَ خَطَايَا حَوْلَيْهِ أَحَبُّ إِلَيَّ مِنْ أَنْ أَعْمَلَ هَهُنَا خَطِيئَةً وَاحِدَةً

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki‘ → Mis‘ar → ‘Amr ibn Murrah → Talq ibn Habib → ‘Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki‘ narrated to us, from Mis‘ar, from ‘Amr ibn Murrah, from Talq ibn Habib, who said: ‘Umar said: "O people of Makkah, fear Allah regarding Allah's sanctuary. Do you know who used to inhabit this House? Banu so-and-so were in it, they violated its sanctity and were destroyed. And Banu so-and-so were in it, they violated its sanctity and were destroyed." Until he mentioned whatever Arab tribes he wished to mention. Then he said: "That I commit ten sins outside of it is dearer to me than committing a single sin here."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنِ السُّدِّيِّ، عَنْ مُرَّةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ: "مَنْ هَمَّ بِسَيِّئَةٍ لَمْ تُكْتَبْ عَلَيْهِ حَتَّى يَعْمَلَهَا، وَإِنْ هَمَّ وَهُوَ بِعَدَنِ، أَبْيَنَ أَنْ يَقْتُلَ عِنْدَ الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ، أَذَاقَهُ اللَّهُ مِنْ عَذَابٍ أَلِيمٍ ثُمَّ قَرَأَ: {وَمَنْ يُرِدْ فِيهِ بِإِلْحَادٍ} [الحج: 25] بِظُلْمٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki‘ → Sufyan → As-Suddi → Murrah → ‘Abdullah
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki‘ narrated to us, from Sufyan, from As-Suddi, from Murrah, from ‘Abdullah, who said: "Whoever intends a bad deed, it is not written against him until he does it. But if one intends it [profanity/injustice in the Haram] even while he is in Aden, it is clear that he would be killed near the Sacred Mosque, Allah will make him taste a painful punishment." Then he recited: {And whoever intends therein [a deed] of deviation} [Al-Hajj: 25] meaning injustice.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ أَبِي سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو قَالَ: إِنَّ الْحَرَمَ مُحَرَّمٌ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ السَّبْعِ مِقْدَارُهُ مِنَ الْأَرْضِ، وَإِنَّ الْبَيْتَ الْمُقَدَّسَ فِي السَّمَوَاتِ السَّبْعِ مِقْدَارُهُ مِنَ الْأَرْضِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Mu‘awiyah → Al-A‘mash → Abu Sulayman → ‘Abdullah ibn ‘Amr
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Abu Mu‘awiyah narrated to us, from Al-A‘mash, from Abu Sulayman, from ‘Abdullah ibn ‘Amr, who said: "Indeed, the Sanctuary (Haram) is sanctified in the seven heavens just as it is on earth, and the Sanctified House (Bayt al-Maqdis) is in the seven heavens just as it is on earth."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ أَبِي زِيَادٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ سَابِطٍ قَالَ: كَانَ النَّاسُ إِذَا كَانَ الْمَوْسِمُ بِالْجَاهِلِيَّةِ خَرَجُوا، فَلَمْ يَبْقَ أَحَدٌ بِمَكَّةَ، وَإِنَّهُ تَخَلَّفَ رَجُلٌ سَارِقٌ فَعَمَدَ إِلَى قِطْعَةٍ مِنْ ذَهَبٍ فَوَضَعَهَا، ثُمَّ دَخَلَ لِيَأْخُذَ أَيْضًا فَلَمَّا أَدْخَلَ رَأْسَهُ ضَرَّهُ الْبَيْتُ، فَوَجَدُوا رَأْسَهُ فِي الْبَيْتِ، وَاسْتَهُ خَارِجَهُ، فَأَلْقَوْهُ لِلْكِلَابِ وَأَصْلَحُوا الْبَيْتَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Fudayl → Yazid ibn Abi Ziyad → Ibn Sabit
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Fudayl narrated to us, from Yazid ibn Abi Ziyad, from Ibn Sabit, who said: "In the Time of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah), when the season arrived, the people would leave, and no one would remain in Makkah. A thief stayed behind and aimed for a piece of gold, so he put it [aside], then he entered to take more. When he put his head in, the House squeezed him. They found his head inside the House and his backside outside it. They threw him to the dogs and repaired the House."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو، أَنَّهُ كَانَ لَهُ فُسْطَاطَانِ أَحَدُهُمَا فِي الْحَرَمِ وَالْآخَرُ فِي الْحِلِّ، فَإِذَا أَرَادَ أَنْ يُصَلِّيَ صَلَّى فِي الَّذِي فِي الْحَرَمِ، وَإِذَا كَانَتْ لَهُ الْحَاجَةُ إِلَى أَهْلِهِ جَاءَ إِلَى الَّذِي فِي الْحِلِّ، فَقِيلَ لَهُ فِي ذَلِكَ، فَقَالَ: إِنَّ مَكَّةَ مَكَّةُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ghundar → Shu‘bah → Mansur → Mujahid → ‘Abdullah ibn ‘Amr
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ghundar narrated to us, from Shu‘bah, from Mansur, from Mujahid, from ‘Abdullah ibn ‘Amr, that he had two tents: one in the Haram and the other in the Hill (outside the Haram). When he wanted to pray, he prayed in the one in the Haram, and when he had a need with his family, he went to the one in the Hill. He was asked about that, and he said: "Indeed, Makkah is Makkah (sacred)."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْوَهَّابِ، عَنْ خَالِدٍ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لَهُ: مَا لَا يُنَفَّرُ صَيْدُهَا؟ قَالَ: تُحَوِّلُهُ مِنَ الظِّلِّ، وَالشَّمْسِ، وَتَنْزِلُ مَكَانَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → ‘Abd al-Wahhab → Khalid → ‘Ikrimah
english.text: Abu Bakr said: ‘Abd al-Wahhab narrated to us, from Khalid, from ‘Ikrimah, who said: I said to him: "What does 'its game is not chased' mean?" He said: "You move it from the shade to the sun and take its place."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ بْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنْ زِيَادِ بْنِ سَعْدٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ، سَمِعَ أَبَا هُرَيْرَةَ يَقُولُ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: يُخَرِّبُ الْكَعْبَةَ ذُو السُّوَيْقَتَيْنِ مِنَ الْحَبَشَةِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Sufyan ibn ‘Uyaynah → Ziyad ibn Sa‘d → Az-Zuhri → Sa‘id ibn Al-Musayyib → Abu Hurayrah
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Sufyan ibn ‘Uyaynah narrated to us, from Ziyad ibn Sa‘d, from Az-Zuhri, from Sa‘id ibn Al-Musayyib, he heard Abu Hurayrah saying, from the Prophet ﷺ: "The Kaaba will be destroyed by Dhul-Suwayqatayn (the one with thin legs) from Abyssinia."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: إِسْحَاقُ الْأَزْرَقُ: عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ حَسَّانَ، عَنْ حَفْصَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي الْعَالِيَةِ، عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ قَالَ: كَأَنِّي أَنْظُرُ إِلَى رَجُلٍ مِنَ الْحَبَشِ أَصْلَعَ أَصْمَعَ حَمْشِ السَّاقَيْنِ جَالِسٍ عَلَيْهَا وَهُوَ يَهْدِمُهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ishaq Al-Azraq → Hisham ibn Hassan → Hafsah → Abu Al-‘Aliyah → ‘Ali ibn Abi Talib
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ishaq Al-Azraq [narrated to us]: from Hisham ibn Hassan, from Hafsah, from Abu Al-‘Aliyah, from ‘Ali ibn Abi Talib, who said: "It is as if I am looking at a man from Abyssinia, bald, with small ears and thin legs, sitting on it while demolishing it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي نَجِيحٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، سَمِعَ ابْنَ عَمْرٍو يَقُولُ: كَأَنِّي بِهِ أَصْلَعَ، أُفَيْدِعَ، قَائِمٍ عَلَيْهَا يَهْدِمُهَا بِمِسْحَاتِهِ، فَلَمَّا هَدَمَهَا ابْنُ الزُّبَيْرِ جَعَلْتُ أَنْظُرُ إِلَى صِفَةِ ابْنِ عَمْرٍو فَلَمْ أَرَهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn ‘Uyaynah → Ibn Abi Najih → Mujahid → Ibn ‘Amr
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn ‘Uyaynah narrated to us, from Ibn Abi Najih, from Mujahid, he heard Ibn ‘Amr saying: "It is as if I see him, bald, bow-legged, standing on it demolishing it with his spade." When Ibn Az-Zubayr demolished it, I started looking for the description given by Ibn ‘Amr, but I did not see it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنْ دَاوُدَ بْنِ شَابُورَ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ قَالَ: لَمَّا أَجْمَعَ ابْنُ الزُّبَيْرِ عَلَى هَدْمِهَا خَرَجْنَا إِلَى مِنًى ثَلَاثًا نَنْتَظِرُ الْعَذَابَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn ‘Uyaynah → Dawud ibn Shabur → Mujahid
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn ‘Uyaynah narrated to us, from Dawud ibn Shabur, from Mujahid, who said: "When Ibn Az-Zubayr decided to demolish it, we went out to Mina for three days waiting for the punishment."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ سَلَمَةَ بْنِ كُهَيْلٍ، عَنْ أَبِي صَادِقٍ، عَنْ حَسَنٍ الْكِنَانِيِّ، عَنْ عَلِيمٍ الْكِنْدِيِّ، عَنْ سَلْمَانَ قَالَ: لَيُحْرَقَنَّ هَذَا الْبَيْتُ عَلَى يَدَيْ رَجُلٍ مِنَ آلِ الزُّبَيْرِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki‘ → Sufyan → Salamah ibn Kuhayl → Abu Sadiq → Hasan Al-Kinani → ‘Alim Al-Kindi → Salman
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki‘ narrated to us, from Sufyan, from Salamah ibn Kuhayl, from Abu Sadiq, from Hasan Al-Kinani, from ‘Alim Al-Kindi, from Salman, who said: "This House will surely be burned at the hands of a man from the family of Az-Zubayr."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا الْأَعْمَشُ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ قَالَ: "لَمَّا هُدِمَ الْبَيْتُ وُجِدَ فِيهِ صَخْرَةٌ مَكْتُوبٌ فِيهَا: أَنَا اللَّهُ ذُو بَكَّةَ صُغْتُهُ يَوْمَ صُغْتُ الشَّمْسَ وَالْقَمَرَ، حَفَفْتُهُ بِسَبْعَةِ أَمْلَاكٍ حِلَقًا، بَارَكْتُ لِأَهْلِهِ فِي السَّمْنِ وَالسَّمِينِ، لَا يَزُولُ حَتَّى تَزُولَ الْأَخْشَبَانِ، يَعْنِي الْجَبَلَيْنِ،، وَأَوَّلُ مَنْ يَحِلُّهَا أَهْلُهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki‘ → Al-A‘mash → Mujahid
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Al-A‘mash narrated to us, from Mujahid, who said: "When the House was demolished, a rock was found in it inscribed: 'I am Allah, Lord of Bakkah. I fashioned it on the day I fashioned the sun and the moon. I surrounded it with seven angels in circles. I blessed its people in meat and milk. It shall not cease until the two mountains (Akhshaban) cease.' And the first to violate its sanctity are its own people."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سَلَمَةُ بْنُ نُبَيْطٍ، عَنِ الضَّحَّاكِ بْنِ مُزَاحِمٍ قَالَ: "لَمَّا كُسِرَ الْبَيْتُ، جَاءَ سَيْلٌ، فَقَلَبَ حَجَرًا مِنْ حِجَارَةِ الْبَيْتِ، فَإِذَا مَكْتُوبٌ فِيهِ: أَنَا اللَّهُ ذُو بَكَّةَ صُغْتُهُ يَوْمَ صُغْتُ الْجَبَلَيْنِ، بَنَيْتُهُ عَلَى وُجُوهِ سَبْعَةِ أَمْلَاكٍ حُنَفَاءَ لَيْسُوا يَهُودًا، وَلَا نَصَارَى

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki‘ → Salamah ibn Nubayt → Ad-Dahhak ibn Muzahim
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Salamah ibn Nubayt narrated to us, from Ad-Dahhak ibn Muzahim, who said: "When the House was broken, a flood came and overturned a stone from the stones of the House. Behold, inscribed on it was: 'I am Allah, Lord of Bakkah. I fashioned it on the day I fashioned the two mountains. I built it upon the faces of seven pure angels (Hanifs), neither Jews nor Christians.'"

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا زَكَرِيَّا، عَنْ عَامِرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي مَنْ، قَرَأَ كِتَابًا فِي سَقْفِ الْبَيْتِ أَوْ أَسْفَلَ مَقَامِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ: أَنَا اللَّهُ ذُو بَكَّةَ بَنَيْتُهُ عَلَى وُجُوهِ سَبْعَةِ أَمْلَاكٍ حُنَفَاءَ، بَارَكْتُ لِأَهْلِهِ فِي اللَّحْمِ وَالْمَاءِ، وَجَعَلْتُ رِزْقَ أَهْلِهِ مِنْ ثَلَاثَةِ سُبُلٍ، وَلَا يَسْتَحِلَّ حُرْمَتَهُ أَوَّلُ مَنْ أَهَلَّهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki‘ → Zakariyya → ‘Amir
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki‘ narrated to us, saying: Zakariyya narrated to us, from ‘Amir, who said: Someone who read a writing in the ceiling of the House or beneath the Maqam of Ibrahim narrated to me: "I am Allah, Lord of Bakkah. I built it upon the faces of seven pure angels (Hanifs). I blessed its people in meat and water, and I made the sustenance of its people come from three paths. And the first to violate its sanctity are those whom I made its people."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي نَجِيحٍ، عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ مِينَاءَ قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ عُمَرَ يَقُولُ: إِذَا رَأَيْتُمْ قُرَيْشًا قَدْ هَدَمُوا الْبَيْتَ ثُمَّ بَنُوهُ، فَإِنِ اسْتَطَعْتَ أَنْ تَمُوتَ فَمُتْ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Ibn Abi Najih → Sulayman ibn Mina’ → ‘Abdullah ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn ‘Ulayyah narrated to us, from Ibn Abi Najih, from Sulayman ibn Mina’, who said: I heard ‘Abdullah ibn ‘Umar say: "If you see Quraysh having demolished the House and then rebuilt it, then if you can die, die."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ يَعْلَى بْنِ عَطَاءٍ قَالَ: كُنْتُ آخُذُ بِلِجَامِ دَابَّةِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ، فَقَالَ: كَيْفَ أَنْتُمْ إِذَا هَدَمْتُمْ هَذَا الْبَيْتَ، فَلَمْ تَدَعُوا حَجَرًا عَلَى حَجَرٍ؟ قَالُوا: وَنَحْنُ عَلَى الْإِسْلَامِ؟ قَالَ: وَنَحْنُ عَلَى الْإِسْلَامِ قَالَ: ثُمَّ مَاذَا؟ قَالَ: ثُمَّ يُبْنَى أَحْسَنَ مَا كَانَ، فَإِذَا رَأَيْتُ مَكَّةَ قَدْ لَعَجَتْ لَطَائِمَ، وَرَأَيْتُ الْبِنَاءَ يَعْلُو رُءُوسَ الْجِبَالِ، فَاعْلَمْ أَنَّ الْأَمْرَ قَدْ أَظَلَّكَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ghundar → Shu‘bah → Ya‘la ibn ‘Ata’ → ‘Abdullah ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ghundar narrated to us, from Shu‘bah, from Ya‘la ibn ‘Ata’, who said: I was holding the bridle of ‘Abdullah ibn ‘Umar's beast, and he said: "How will you be when you demolish this House and leave no stone upon another?" They said: "While we are upon Islam?" He said: "While you are upon Islam." He said: "Then what?" He said: "Then it will be built better than it was. But when you see Makkah's belly has been ripped open (with tunnels/channels) and you see buildings surpassing the tops of the mountains, then know that the command has cast its shadow upon you."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، عَنْ حُمَيْدٍ، عَنْ بَكْرِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْمُزَنِي، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عَمْرٍو قَالَ: تَمَتَّعُوا مِنْ هَذَا الْبَيْتِ قَبْلَ أَنْ يُرْفَعَ، فَإِنَّهُ سَيُرْفَعُ وَيُهْدَمُ مَرَّتَيْنِ، وَيُرْفَعُ فِي الثَّالِثَةِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Yazid ibn Harun → Humayd → Bakr ibn ‘Abdullah Al-Muzani → ‘Abdullah ibn ‘Amr
english.text: Abu Bakr narrated to us: Yazid ibn Harun narrated to us, from Humayd, from Bakr ibn ‘Abdullah Al-Muzani, from ‘Abdullah ibn ‘Amr, who said: "Benefit from this House before it is lifted, for it will be lifted and demolished twice, and lifted on the third."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي مُلَيْكَةَ، عَنْ عَائِشَةَ قَالَتْ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: لَوْ كَانَ عِنْدَنَا سَعَةٌ لَهَدَمْتُ الْكَعْبَةَ وَلَبَنَيْتُهَا، وَجَعَلْتُ لَهَا بَابَيْنِ، بَابًا يَدْخُلُ مِنْهُ النَّاسُ، وَبَابًا يَخْرُجُونَ مِنْهُ فَلَمَّا وَلِيَ ابْنُ الزُّبَيْرِ هَدَمَهَا، فَجَعَلَ لَهَا بَابَيْنِ، فَكَانَتْ كَذَلِكَ، فَلَمَّا ظَهَرَ الْحَجَّاجُ عَلَيْهِ، هَدَمَهَا وَأَعَادَ بِنَاءَهَا الْأَوَّلَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki‘ → Isma‘il ibn ‘Abd al-Malik → Ibn Abi Mulaykah → ‘A’ishah
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki‘ narrated to us, from Isma‘il ibn ‘Abd al-Malik, from Ibn Abi Mulaykah, from ‘A’ishah, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "If we had the means, I would have demolished the Kaaba and rebuilt it, and I would have made two doors for it: a door for people to enter and a door for them to exit." When Ibn Az-Zubayr took authority, he demolished it and made two doors for it, so it was like that. When Al-Hajjaj prevailed over him, he demolished it and restored it to its first construction.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ أَبِي بَكْرٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ أَبِي الْبَدَّاحِ بْنِ عَدِيٍّ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ رَخَّصَ لِلرِّعَاءِ أَنْ يَرْمُوا يَوْمًا وَيَرْعَوْا يَوْمًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn ‘Uyaynah → ‘Abdullah ibn Abi Bakr → His father → Abu Al-Baddah ibn ‘Adi → His father
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn ‘Uyaynah narrated to us, from ‘Abdullah ibn Abi Bakr, from his father, from Abu Al-Baddah ibn ‘Adi, from his father, "that the Prophet ﷺ granted a concession to the shepherds to throw [pebbles] one day and pasture one day."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، أَنَّ النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ رَخَّصَ لِلرِّعَاءِ أَنْ يَرْمُوا لَيْلًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn ‘Uyaynah → Ibn Jurayj → ‘Ata’
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn ‘Uyaynah narrated to us, from Ibn Jurayj, from ‘Ata’, "that the Prophet ﷺ granted a concession to the shepherds to throw at night."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَمَّادُ بْنُ خَالِدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي ذِئْبٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، أَنَّ عُمَرَ رَخَّصَ لِلرِّعَاءِ أَنْ يَبِيتُوا عَنْ مِنًى قَالَ: فَذَكَرْتُ ذَلِكَ لِإِبْرَاهِيمَ، وَلِلزُّهْرِيِّ، فَقَالَا: الرِّعَاءُ يَرْمُونَ لَيْلًا، وَلَا يَبِيتُونَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Hammad ibn Khalid → Ibn Abi Dhi’b → ‘Ata’
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Hammad ibn Khalid narrated to us, from Ibn Abi Dhi’b, from ‘Ata’, "that ‘Umar granted a concession to the shepherds to spend the night away from Mina." He said: I mentioned that to Ibrahim and Az-Zuhri, and they said: "The shepherds throw at night and do not spend the night [at Mina]."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ نُمَيْرٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَجْعَلُ رَمْيَ الْجِمَارِ نَوَائِبَ بَيْنَ رِعَاءِ الْإِبِلِ، يَأْمُرُ الَّذِي عِنْدَهُ، فَيَرْمُونَ إِذَا زَالَتِ الشَّمْسُ، ثُمَّ يَذْهَبُونَ إِلَى الْإِبِلِ، وَيَأْتِي الَّذِينَ فِي الْإِبِلِ فَيَرْمُونَ، ثُمَّ يَمْكُثُونَ حَتَّى يَرْمُونَهَا مِنَ الْغَدِ إِذَا زَالَتِ الشَّمْسُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Numayr → ‘Abdullah → Nafi‘ → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Numayr narrated to us, from ‘Abdullah, from Nafi‘, from Ibn ‘Umar, "that he used to make the throwing of the pebbles alternate among the camel shepherds. He would order the one who was with him, so they would throw when the sun passed its zenith, then go to the camels. Then those who were with the camels would come and throw, then they would stay until they threw on the next day when the sun passed its zenith."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: يَرْكَبُ الْمَاشِي إِذَا رَمَى الْجَمْرَةَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Sharik → Muhammad ibn Abi Isma‘il → Sa‘id ibn Jubayr → Ibn ‘Abbas
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Sharik narrated to us, from Muhammad ibn Abi Isma‘il, from Sa‘id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn ‘Abbas, who said: "The walker rides when he throws at the Jamrah."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سُلَيْمٍ الطَّائِفِيُّ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ مُجَاهِدًا، وَسَعِيدَ بْنَ جُبَيْرٍ، يَقُولَانِ: كُنَّا نَرَى عَبْدَ اللَّهِ إِذَا رَمَى الْجَمْرَةَ يَرْفَعُ يَدَيْهِ حَتَّى يُسَاوِيَ رَأْسَهُ، وَيُرَى بَيَاضُ إِبْطَيْهِ، وَكَانَ حَصَاهُ مِثْلَ بُنْدُقَةِ الْحَادِرَةِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Yahya ibn Sulaym At-Ta’ifi → ‘Abdullah ibn ‘Uthman → Mujahid & Sa‘id ibn Jubayr
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Yahya ibn Sulaym At-Ta’ifi narrated to us, from ‘Abdullah ibn ‘Uthman, who said: I heard Mujahid and Sa‘id ibn Jubayr saying: "We used to see ‘Abdullah, when he threw at the Jamrah, raise his hands until they were level with his head, and the whiteness of his armpit could be seen. His pebbles were like the hazelnut of a slingshot."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ خَيْثَمٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ قَالَ: إِذَا رَمَى الْجَمْرَةَ فَلْيَرْفَعْ يَدَيْهِ حَتَّى يُرَى بَيَاضُ إِبْطَيْهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Yahya ibn Sa‘id → Ibn Jurayj → Ibn Khaytham → Mujahid
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Yahya ibn Sa‘id narrated to us, from Ibn Jurayj, from Ibn Khaytham, from Mujahid, who said: "When he throws at the Jamrah, let him raise his hands until the whiteness of his armpit is seen."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا كَثِيرُ بْنُ هِشَامٍ، عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ بُرْقَانَ قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنِي الْوَلِيدُ بْنُ دِينَارٍ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ إِذَا رَمَى الْجَمْرَةَ تَقَدَّمَ أَمَامَهَا، فَدَعَا اللَّهَ، وَرَفَعَ يَدَيْهِ وَرَفَعْنَا مَعَهُ، فَمَا يَضَعُ يَدَيْهِ حَتَّى يُهِلَّ، وَنَضَعَ أَيْدِينَا وَهُوَ كَمَا هُوَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Kathir ibn Hisham → Ja‘far ibn Burqan → Al-Walid ibn Dinar → Nafi‘ → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Kathir ibn Hisham narrated to us, from Ja‘far ibn Burqan, who said: Al-Walid ibn Dinar informed me, from Nafi‘, from Ibn ‘Umar, "that when he threw at the Jamrah, he would move forward in front of it, supplicate to Allah, and raise his hands, and we would raise [our hands] with him. He would not put down his hands until he moved away (Yuhill?), and we would keep our hands [raised] as long as he was like that."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ يَقُولُونَ: تُرْفَعُ الْأَيْدِي عِنْدَ الْجَمْرَتَيْنِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Fudayl → ‘Ata’ → Sa‘id ibn Jubayr → Ibn ‘Abbas
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Fudayl narrated to us, from ‘Ata’, from Sa‘id ibn Jubayr, from Ibn ‘Abbas, who used to say: "Hands are raised at the two Jamrahs."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، عَنْ مِقْسَمٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، وَعَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ قَالَ: لَا تُرْفَعُ الْأَيْدِي عِنْدَ الْجِمَارِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Mu‘awiyah → Ibn Abi Layla → Al-Hakam → Miqsam → Ibn ‘Abbas
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Abu Mu‘awiyah narrated to us, from Ibn Abi Layla, from Al-Hakam, from Miqsam, from Ibn ‘Abbas; and from Ibn Abi Layla, from Nafi‘, from Ibn ‘Umar, who said: "Hands are not raised at the Jamarat."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ الْمُبَارَكِ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، فِي الرَّجُلِ يَحُجُّ فَيَمُوتُ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَقْضِيَ نُسُكَهُ قَالَ: يُقْضَى عَنْهُ مَا بَقِيَ مِنْ نُسُكِهِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Al-Mubarak → Hisham → Al-Hasan
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Al-Mubarak narrated to us, from Hisham, from Al-Hasan, regarding a man who performs Hajj and dies before completing his rituals. He said: "What remains of his rituals is performed on his behalf."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ أَبِي نَهِيكٍ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ طَاوُسًا، عَنِ امْرَأَةٍ تُوُفِّيَتْ، وَقَدْ بَقِيَ عَلَيْهَا مِنْ نُسُكِهَا؟ قَالَ: يُقْضَى عَنْهَا وَسَأَلْتُ الْقَاسِمَ فَقَالَ: "لَا عِلْمَ لِي بِمَا قَالَ: طَاوُسٌ قَالَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى: {لَا تَزِرُ وَازِرَةٌ وِزْرَ أُخْرَى} [الأنعام: 164]

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Mahdi → Sufyan → Abu Nahik → Tawus
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Mahdi narrated to us, from Sufyan, from Abu Nahik, who said: I asked Tawus about a woman who died while some of her rituals remained? He said: "It is performed on her behalf." I asked Al-Qasim, and he said: "I have no knowledge of what Tawus said. Allah the Exalted said: {No bearer of burdens will bear the burden of another} [Al-An‘am: 164]."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ حُصَيْنٍ، عَنْ أَبِي مَالِكٍ قَالَ: مَوْضِعُ الْبَيْتِ مَكَّةُ، وَمَا سِوَى ذَلِكَ بَكَّةُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Fudayl → Husayn → Abu Malik
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Ibn Fudayl narrated to us, from Husayn, from Abu Malik, who said: "The location of the House is Makkah, and whatever is other than that is Bakkah."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا كَثِيرُ بْنُ هِشَامٍ، عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ بُرْقَانَ قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ عِكْرِمَةَ يَقُولُ: بَكَّةُ مَا حَوْلَ الْبَيْتِ، وَمَكَّةُ مَا وَرَاءَ ذَلِكَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Kathir ibn Hisham → Ja‘far ibn Burqan → ‘Ikrimah
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Kathir ibn Hisham narrated to us, from Ja‘far ibn Burqan, who said: I heard ‘Ikrimah say: "Bakkah is what is around the House, and Makkah is what is beyond that."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنِ الْأَسْوَدِ بْنِ قَيْسٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنِ ابْنِ الزُّبَيْرِ قَالَ: إِنَّمَا سُمِّيَتْ بَكَّةَ لِأَنَّ النَّاسَ يَجِيئُونَ مِنْ كُلِّ جَانِبٍ حُجَّاجًا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki‘ → Sufyan → Al-Aswad ibn Qays → His father → Ibn Az-Zubayr
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki‘ narrated to us, from Sufyan, from Al-Aswad ibn Qays, from his father, from Ibn Az-Zubayr, who said: "It was named Bakkah only because people crowd (come) from every side as pilgrims."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ سَعِيدَ بْنَ جُبَيْرٍ، وَسُئِلَ، لِمَ سُمِّيَتْ بَكَّةَ،؟ قَالَ: لِأَنَّهُمْ يَتَبَاكَوْنَ فِيهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Waki‘ → Sufyan → Hammad → Sa‘id ibn Jubayr
english.text: Abu Bakr said: Waki‘ narrated to us, from Sufyan, from Hammad, who said: I heard Sa‘id ibn Jubayr being asked: "Why was it named Bakkah?" He said: "Because they crowd (Yatabakawn - push one another) in it."

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ قَالَ: إِنَّمَا سُمِّيَتْ بَكَّةَ لِأَنَّ النَّاسَ يَتَبَاكَوْنَ بِهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Mahdī → ‘Amr ibn Shu‘ayb
english.text: ‘Amr ibn Shu‘ayb said: “It was named Bakkah only because the people crowd together (yatabakkawna) in it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا جَعْفَرُ بْنُ عَوْنٍ، عَنْ مِسْعَرٍ، عَنْ عُتْبَةَ بْنِ قَيْسٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ قَالَ: إِنَّ مَكَّةَ بَكَتْ بُكَاءً الذَّكَرُ فِيهَا كَالْأُنْثَى

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ja‘far ibn ‘Awn → Mis‘ar → ‘Utbah ibn Qays → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Ibn ‘Umar said: “Mecca crowded (bakkat) such a crowding that the male in it was like the female (in crowding).”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ قَالَ: إِنَّمَا سُمِّيَتْ بَكَّةَ لِأَنَّ النَّاسَ يَبُكُّ بَعْضُهُمْ بَعْضًا فِيهَا، وَإِنَّهُ يَحِلُّ فِيهَا مَا لَا يَحِلُّ فِي غَيْرِهَا

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Yazīd ibn Hārūn → Ḥajjāj → Al-Ḥakam → Mujāhid
english.text: Mujāhid said: “It was named Bakkah only because the people crowd (yabukku) one another in it, and because things are permissible in it that are not permissible elsewhere (referring to passing in front of someone praying, etc., in crowding).”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ عَطِيَّةَ قَالَ: بَكَّةُ مَوْضِعُ الْبَيْتِ، وَمَا حَوْلَهُ مَكَّةُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Wakī‘ → Fuḍayl → ‘Aṭiyyah
english.text: ‘Aṭiyyah said: “Bakkah is the site of the House (Ka‘bah), and what is around it is Mecca.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَزِيدُ بْنُ هَارُونَ، عَنِ التَّيْمِيِّ، عَنْ أَبِي مِجْلَزٍ، "أَنَّ جَبْرَائِيلَ أَتَى بِإِبْرَاهِيمَ عَرَفَاتٍ، فَقَالَ: عَرَفْتَ؟ قَالَ: نَعَمْ، قَالَ: فَمِنْ ثَمَّ سُمِّيَتْ عَرَفَاتٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Yazīd ibn Hārūn → Al-Taymī → Abu Mijlaz
english.text: Abu Mijlaz said: “Gabriel brought Abraham to ‘Arafat and said: ‘Do you know (it)?’ He said: ‘Yes.’ He said: ‘From there it was named ‘Arafat.’”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَعْلَى بْنُ عُبَيْدٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ قَالَ: "إِنَّمَا سُمِّيَتْ عَرَفَاتٍ لِأَنَّ جِبْرِيلَ كَانَ يُرِي إِبْرَاهِيمَ الْمَنَاسِكَ فَيَقُولُ: عَرَفْتَ؟ ثُمَّ يُرِيهِ فَيَقُولُ: عَرَفْتَ؟ فَسُمِّيَتْ عَرَفَاتٍ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ya‘lā ibn ‘Ubayd → ‘Abd al-Malik → ‘Aṭā’
english.text: ‘Aṭā’ said: “It was named ‘Arafat only because Gabriel used to show Abraham the rituals and say: ‘Do you know?’ Then he would show him (another) and say: ‘Do you know?’ So it was named ‘Arafat.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ الْمُغِيرَةِ، عَنْ حُمَيْدِ بْنِ هِلَالٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الصَّامِتِ، عَنْ أَبِي ذَرٍّ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: إِنَّهَا مُبَارَكَةٌ، يَعْنِي زَمْزَمَ، طَعَامُ مَنْ طَعِمَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Abu Usāmah → Sulaymān ibn al-Mughīrah → Ḥumayd ibn Hilāl → ‘Abd Allāh ibn al-Ṣāmit → Abu Dharr
english.text: Abu Dharr reported that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: “It is blessed (meaning Zamzam), it is food that nourishes.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، أَنَّهُ قَالَ فِي مَاءِ زَمْزَمَ: طَعَامُ مَنْ طَعِمَ، وَشِفَاءُ مَنْ سَقِمَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Wakī‘ → Ibn Abī Laylā → Ibn ‘Abbās
english.text: Ibn ‘Abbās said regarding Zamzam water: “It is food that nourishes and a cure for the sick.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنِ الْعَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي الْعَبَّاسِ، عَنْ أَبِي الطُّفَيْلِ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ قَالَ: كُنَّا نُسَمِّي زَمْزَمَ شَبَّاعَةَ، وَنَزْعُمُ أَنَّهَا نِعْمَ الْعَوْنُ عَلَى الْعِيَالِ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Al-‘Alī ibn Abī al-‘Abbās → Abu al-Ṭufayl → Ibn ‘Abbās
english.text: Ibn ‘Abbās said: “We used to call Zamzam ‘Shabba‘ah’ (Satisfying), and we claimed it was an excellent help for the dependents (family).”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ فُضَيْلٍ، عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ أَبِي زِيَادٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي رَجُلٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ الشَّامِ، عَنْ كَعْبٍ قَالَ: سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ: إِنَّ فِي كِتَابِ اللَّهِ الْمُنَزَّلِ أَنَّ مَاءَ زَمْزَمَ طَعَامُ مَنْ طَعِمَ، وَشِفَاءُ مَنْ سَقِمَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Ibn Fuḍayl → Yazīd ibn Abī Ziyād → A man from the people of Sham → Ka‘b
english.text: Ka‘b said: “In the revealed Book of Allah, it is stated that Zamzam water is food that nourishes and a cure for the sick.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنَا أَبُو إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ قَيْسِ بْنِ كُرْكُمَ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ ابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ فَقُلْتُ: أَخْبِرْنِي عَنْ مَاءِ زَمْزَمَ فَقَالَ: أَخْبِرْنِي بِعِلْمٍ لَا تَنْزَحُ، وَلَا تَنْزِفُ، وَلَا تُزَمُّ، طَعَامُ مَنْ طَعِمَ، وَشِفَاءُ مَنْ سَقِمَ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Yaḥyā ibn Sa‘īd → Sufyān → Abu Isḥāq → Qays ibn Kurkum → Ibn ‘Abbās
english.text: Qays ibn Kurkum said: I asked Ibn ‘Abbās: “Tell me about Zamzam water.” He said: “Tell me with knowledge: It does not dry up, nor is it exhausted, nor is it curbed. It is food that nourishes and a cure for the sick.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا سَعِيدُ بْنُ زَكَرِيَّا، وَزَيْدُ بْنُ الْحُبَابِ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الْمُؤَمَّلِ، عَنْ أَبِي الزُّبَيْرِ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: مَاءُ زَمْزَمَ لِمَا شُرِبَ لَهُ

english.narrator: Abu Bakr → Sa‘īd ibn Zakariyyā and Zayd ibn al-Ḥubāb → ‘Abd Allāh ibn al-Mu’ammal → Abu al-Zubayr → Jābir
